Patch Set 1:

It gives back the ability to lnetctl ping hostanme@nettype. Yes its using libcfs_nidstr() so only 1 IP address, the perfered one, is returned. 

In theory we can update this function to use some form of .nf_parse_addrlist() backend to get all the addresses but would be always want to get every address every time? For example in this ping case do we want to ping every address everytime for a hostanme@nettype?

parents.subject: LU-17722 tests: trim tmpfs from wait_delete_completed()

commit.subject: LU-17629 utils: support hostname with  lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range()
commit.message:
LU-17629 utils: support hostname with
 lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range()

For a hostname it's possible it maps to multiple IPs. In
this case lnetctl commands that attempt to use the hostname
can resolve to the wrong IP address. Update the function
lustre_lnet_parse_nid_range() to work with hostnames and
properly resolve the correct IP address. Update both
lnetctl ping and lnetctl discover to work with
lnet_parse_nid_range().
